free_text: Cross Sections for (n,2n), (n,p) and (n,a) Reactions
on Rare-earth Isotopes at 14.7 MeV
ZF-300-II Intense Neutron Generator
Deuteron-Tritium reaction with an effective
deuteron beam energy of 125 KeV and beam current of
20 mA. The Tritium-Titanium (T-Ti) target used in
generator was 0.9mg/cm2 thick.
The groups of samples were placed at 0 degree angles
relative to the beam direction and centred about
the (T-Ti) target at distances of approx. 5 to 8 cm.
Samples of 20mm diameter were made of spectroscopically
pure rare-earth oxides and 99.99 percent purity
natural metal foils of aluminium, niobium and
zirconium.
Gamma ray activity was determined by CH8403
coaxial high-purity detector with a relative efficiency
of 20 percent and an energy resolution of 3 KeV at
Errors are given with no explanation.
Data from table 1 and 2 of Appl.Radiat.Isot.Vol.49
VZ
Taken from E.Brown, R.B.Firestone,
Table of Radioactive Isotopes, Wiley, New York, 1986.
